description:
You are a Tier-1 support engineer and have just pulled two raw performance snapshots from a customer
    system.  
    Both snapshots are plain text, single-space separated, and already sit on disk:

    1. /home/user/diag/cpu_usage.log  
       • column-1  ISO-8601 timestamp  
       • column-2  average CPU utilisation in %

    2. /home/user/diag/mem_usage.log  
       • column-1  ISO-8601 timestamp (identical order and count as above)  
       • column-2  resident memory usage in MB

    Management wants a single, comma-separated spreadsheet-ready file that merges the two logs
    on their common timestamp.  
    Produce that file as /home/user/diag/combined_usage.csv with the exact format shown below
    (including the header row and the final trailing newline character):

        timestamp,cpu_percent,mem_mb
        2023-07-15T10:00:00,12,2048
        2023-07-15T10:05:00,18,2100
        2023-07-15T10:10:00,20,2150
        2023-07-15T10:15:00,25,2200

    Requirements and constraints
    • Use standard GNU coreutils only (e.g. cut, paste, echo/redirection).  
    • Try to accomplish everything in a simple pipeline of no more than 3–4 commands.  
    • Do not re-type the raw numbers manually; extract them from the provided files.  
    • After creating the file, display its contents in the terminal so that a reviewer can immediately see
      the final result.

    An automated test will verify:
    1. /home/user/diag/combined_usage.csv exists.  
    2. The file’s full content is byte-for-byte identical to the block above, including the header and the
       terminating newline.  
    3. The agent printed the same content to stdout during the session.

    You have normal user privileges and write permission to /home/user/diag/.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing diagnostics -->
    <preexisting>
        <file path="/home/user/diag/cpu_usage.log"><![CDATA[
2023-07-15T10:00:00 12
2023-07-15T10:05:00 18
2023-07-15T10:10:00 20
2023-07-15T10:15:00 25
]]></file>
        <file path="/home/user/diag/mem_usage.log"><![CDATA[
2023-07-15T10:00:00 2048
2023-07-15T10:05:00 2100
2023-07-15T10:10:00 2150
2023-07-15T10:15:00 2200
]]></file>
    </preexisting>

    <!-- Verification target created by the agent -->
    <expected>
        <file path="/home/user/diag/combined_usage.csv"><![CDATA[
timestamp,cpu_percent,mem_mb
2023-07-15T10:00:00,12,2048
2023-07-15T10:05:00,18,2100
2023-07-15T10:10:00,20,2150
2023-07-15T10:15:00,25,2200
]]></file>
    </expected>
